questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Hybridisation is a process of mixing of atomic orbitals to give mixed or hybrid orbitals. Hybrid orbitals have equal energy and their hypothetical shape may be given as
Five d-orbitals are non-degenerate, they are divided into two different set of orbitals $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{y}^2,} \mathrm{~d}_{\mathrm{z}^2} \\ & \mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{xy}}, \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{y}^2}, \mathrm{~d}_{\mathrm{zx}} \end{aligned}$ Hybridisation involving d-orbitals are $d s p^2, s p^3 d, d s p^3, s p^3 d^2, d^2 s^3, s p^3 d^3$Question: Which of the following orbitals are involved in $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$ hybridisation?
questions.solution: $s p^3 d^2 \rightarrow$ it involves $d_{x^2-y^2}$ and $d_{z^2}$ orbitals in hybridisation.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Hybridisation is a process of mixing of atomic orbitals to give mixed or hybrid orbitals. Hybrid orbitals have equal energy and their hypothetical shape may be given as
Five d-orbitals are non-degenerate, they are divided into two different set of orbitals $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{y}^2,} \mathrm{~d}_{\mathrm{z}^2} \\ & \mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{xy}}, \mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{y}^2}, \mathrm{~d}_{\mathrm{zx}} \end{aligned}$ Hybridisation involving d-orbitals are $d s p^2, s p^3 d, d s p^3, s p^3 d^2, d^2 s^3, s p^3 d^3$Question: Which of the following d - orbitals are involved in $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^3$ hybridisation?
questions.solution: $s p^3 d^3 \rightarrow$ It involves $d_{x y}, d_{y z}, d_{z x} d$-orbtials in hybridisation.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
